---
title: "How to Create Your Survey in Microsoft Forms or Google Forms"
format: pdf
---

# How to Create Your Survey in Microsoft Forms or Google Forms

This guide provides step-by-step instructions for uploading your survey to online form platforms.

---

## Option 1: Microsoft Forms (Recommended - Uses AI Import)

### Method A: Using Quick Import (Easiest)

**Step 1: Prepare Your Document**

1. Open Microsoft Word
2. Copy the survey content from `training_feedback_survey.md` or use the PDF version
3. Paste it into a new Word document
4. Save the Word document to your computer

**Step 2: Import into Microsoft Forms**

1. Go to [forms.microsoft.com](https://forms.microsoft.com) and sign in with your Microsoft account
2. Click on **Quick Import** (located near the top left of the page)
3. Select **Upload from this device**
4. Choose your Word document (.docx)
5. Select **Form** (not Quiz) when prompted
6. Click **Import**

**Step 3: Review and Edit**

1. Microsoft Forms will automatically create questions from your document
2. Review each question and fix any issues:
   - Change "Scale" questions to **Linear Scale** type (1-5)
   - Change checkbox lists to **Checkbox** type
   - Add section titles if needed
3. Click on each question to edit options

**Step 4: Publish**

1. Click the **Share** button at the top right
2. Copy the link to share with your students
3. Or click **Collect responses** to get a QR code or embed code

---

### Method B: Manual Creation in Microsoft Forms

**Step 1: Create a Blank Form**

1. Go to [forms.microsoft.com](https://forms.microsoft.com)
2. Click **+ New form** (blank form)
3. Enter the title: "AI Training Feedback Survey"

**Step 2: Add Questions**

For each question in your survey:

1. Click **+ Add question**
2. Choose the question type:
   - **Choice** = Multiple choice (radio buttons)
   - **Checkbox** = Select multiple options
   - **Text** = Open-ended short answer
   - **Rating** = Stars (not suitable for 1-5 scale)
   - **Linear Scale** = Numbered scale (1-5, 1-10)

3. Type your question text
4. Add options for choice/checkbox questions

**Step 3: Pro Tips**

- **Copy-Paste Multiple Options:** When adding options like in Question 13, copy the entire list of topics and paste into one option box - Microsoft Forms will automatically split them into separate options!
- **Add Sections:** Use **Section** from the toolbar to organize questions into the 5 sections
- **Required Questions:** Toggle "Required" on if you want students to answer

**Step 4: Share**

1. Click **Share**
2. Copy the link or generate a QR code
3. Send to your students

---

## Option 2: Google Forms

### Method A: Using a Third-Party Import Tool

**Step 1: Use a Free Add-on**

1. Go to [forms.google.com](https://forms.google.com)
2. Create a new blank form
3. Click the three dots (⋮) in the top right
4. Select **Get add-ons**
5. Search for **"Form Publisher"** or **"Form Maker"** (these can import from documents)
6. Install the add-on and follow its instructions to import from your Word/PDF file

### Method B: Manual Creation in Google Forms (Recommended)

**Step 1: Create a Blank Form**

1. Go to [forms.google.com](https://forms.google.com) and sign in
2. Click **+ Blank form**
3. Enter the title: "AI Training Feedback Survey"

**Step 2: Add Sections (Recommended)**

1. Click the **Add section** icon (two horizontal lines with a +) in the right toolbar
2. Create 5 sections matching your survey sections:
   - Section 1: Overall Experience
   - Section 2: Core Comprehension & Confidence
   - Section 3: Course Content Feedback
   - Section 4: Identifying Gaps
   - Section 5: Final Thoughts
3. Add a description to each section

**Step 3: Add Questions**

For each question:

1. Click the **+** icon to add a question
2. Select the question type:
   - **Multiple choice** = Radio buttons (single answer)
   - **Checkboxes** = Multiple selections
   - **Short answer** = Single line text
   - **Paragraph** = Multi-line text
   - **Linear scale** = 1-5 or 1-10 scale (found under "Dropdown" in question types)
   - **Multiple choice grid** = For matrix-style questions

3. Type your question text
4. Add your answer options

**Step 4: The Magic Copy-Paste Trick**

For questions with many options (like Question 13):

1. Copy this list from your survey:
   ```
   Natural Language Processing (NLP) & Large Language Models (LLMs)
   MLOps & Model Deployment (e.g., Docker, AWS, Streamlit/Gradio apps)
   Advanced Computer Vision techniques (Object Detection, Segmentation)
   Time Series Forecasting
   Unsupervised Learning (Clustering, Dimensionality Reduction)
   Data Collection & Web Scraping
   Advanced Agentic AI Workflows
   Other
   ```

2. Click on "Option 1" in Google Forms
3. Press **Ctrl+V** (Paste)
4. Google Forms will automatically create a separate checkbox for each line!

**Step 5: Configure Settings**

1. Click the **Settings** tab (next to Questions)
2. Configure:
   - **Collect email addresses?** (optional)
   - **Allow response editing?** = On
   - **Show progress bar?** = On (helps students see how far they are)

**Step 6: Share**

1. Click the **Send** button (top right)
2. Choose how to share:
   - **Link** = Copy and send via WhatsApp/email
   - **Embed** = Add to a website
   - **Email** = Send directly to email addresses

---

## Quick Comparison

| Feature | Microsoft Forms | Google Forms |
|---------|-----------------|--------------|
| Import from Word/PDF | Yes (Quick Import) | Requires add-on |
| Linear Scale (1-5) | Yes | Yes |
| Sections | Yes | Yes |
| Auto-split pasted options | Yes | Yes |
| Free to use | Yes | Yes |
| Requires Microsoft account | Yes | Yes (Google account) |

---

## Tips for Both Platforms

1. **Test First:** Fill out the survey yourself before sending to students to make sure everything works

2. **Enable Response Collection:**
   - Both platforms automatically collect responses in a spreadsheet
   - In Microsoft Forms: Click "Responses" tab
   - In Google Forms: Click "Responses" tab → Click the Sheets icon to open in Excel/Sheets

3. **Set a Deadline:** Remind students when the survey closes

4. **Share Results (Optional):** Both platforms let you show summary charts after submission

5. **Mobile-Friendly:** Both work great on phones - your students can fill it out on WhatsApp!

---

## Troubleshooting

**Q: The linear scale isn't showing 1-5 properly**
- In Google Forms: Choose "Linear scale" from the question type dropdown, then set min to 1 and max to 5
- In Microsoft Forms: Choose "Linear scale" and set the range 1-5

**Q: The options didn't split when I pasted**
- Make sure each option is on its own line (press Enter between each)
- Delete the existing options first, then paste into "Option 1"

**Q: I can't see the responses**
- Make sure you're signed into the same account that created the form
- Check the "Responses" tab in both platforms

---

*Survey created from: training_feedback_survey.md*
